name: marketplace-workflow-routing
description: Sanitized routing cases for source-owned marketplace, plugin, and primitive workflow decisions.

evidenceRefs: user clarified that invisible generated provider output is not the quality target

cases.prompt: Evaluate overlap between the plugin definitions in the source directory and marketplace, focusing on existing source material, efficacy, overlap, and token waste rather than generated provider drift.

observedRoute.summary: A provider-adapter route can spend the turn diffing generated marketplace payloads instead of auditing canonical source plugins and primitives.
observedRoute.risk: Token budget is wasted on generated churn and the source graph remains ambiguous.

allowedActions: Inspect source/adaptable.marketplace.json, source/plugins, source/skills, source/agents, source/hooks, source/profiles, and garden manifests.
Use materialization only as proof after source quality checks pass.
Add source-owned validation when overlap or routing quality is the failure mode.
forbiddenActions: Patch .agents or .github provider output by hand.
Treat installed plugin cache payloads as canonical source.
Report generated provider drift as the primary answer when the source graph is the target.
cases.recoveryExpectation: If generated output is stale, first make the authored source graph coherent and validated, then regenerate provider output from source only when publication requires it.
cases.notes: This protects the user-stated source ownership boundary and prevents adapter output from hiding source-quality regressions.
cases.productiveOutcome: Source-graph audits spend time on canonical authored material and only touch provider output through regeneration, reducing churn and review noise.
verificationEvidence: The changed files are under source or validation tooling unless provider publication is explicitly in scope.
Validation includes the source graph gate and, when provider output matters, materialization from source.

cases.prompt: Add or revise a plugin composition from canonical primitives, keep the plugin referential, update the marketplace entry, and prove the package validates.

observedRoute.summary: A plugin composition change can be implemented by copying skill payloads into the plugin folder or by relying only on broad source validation.
observedRoute.risk: Primitive ownership becomes hidden inside the plugin and manifest/catalog drift is not caught near the edited surface.

allowedActions: Compose only existing independent primitives with LOCAL_SOURCE ./ references.
Keep primitive payload directories out of source/plugins/<name>.
Run the focused composition helper and the source graph gate.
forbiddenActions: Copy skills, agents, hooks, or concepts into a plugin directory as payload.
Add a plugin manifest without a matching marketplace entry.
Treat installed plugin caches as proof of source composition.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good composition pass should prove manifest/catalog agreement, local primitive paths, standalone skill exposure, and absence of accidental plugin-owned primitive payloads.
cases.notes: This keeps plugin composition as a thin installable surface instead of a second primitive source tree.
cases.productiveOutcome: Referential plugin work remains reviewable and mechanically checked at the composition boundary.
verificationEvidence: The run uses check_plugin_composition for the edited plugin.
The run uses node source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs after source edits.

cases.prompt: Turn a marketplace routing miss into a durable eval case and prove the routing corpus still passes.

observedRoute.summary: A routing miss can be handled as a one-off prompt tweak without adding a sanitized case or replay observation.
observedRoute.risk: The same miss regresses later because no corpus contract exercises the intended primitive route.

allowedActions: Classify one primary routing miss type.
Persist a sanitized schema-backed routing case tied to a canonical primitive.
Run full-corpus replay before claiming routing readiness.
forbiddenActions: Store raw private transcripts or local absolute paths in routing cases.
Patch several trigger surfaces from one miss without evidence.
Keep dead routing fixtures for archaeology after their cases are removed.
cases.recoveryExpectation: A good routing repair should add or update the narrowest case and observation, then prove every checked-in case is still covered by replay.
cases.notes: This makes routing quality a replayed source artifact rather than a memory of a prompt correction.
cases.productiveOutcome: Routing fixes become reproducible corpus improvements with full-corpus coverage evidence.
verificationEvidence: The routing case is schema-backed, sanitized, and points at an existing primitive.
The run includes node source/tools/run-routing-evals.mjs --require-all-observed.

cases.prompt: Make a Kotlin change that needs Kast-backed navigation, Gradle proof, Git hygiene, CI triage, and a pull request.

observedRoute.summary: A broad Kotlin plugin can absorb generic Git, PR, CI, TDD, and shell-safety primitives, duplicating the git-ci-operations plugin.
observedRoute.risk: The same delivery workflow loads twice and the Kotlin trigger becomes too broad for non-Kotlin operational tasks.

allowedActions: Use kotlin-engineering for Kotlin invariants, package cohesion, Kast semantics, Kotlin review, Gradle validation, and Kotlin-specific hooks.
Use git-ci-operations as the companion for branch, commit, PR, CI, release, and shell-safe automation.
Use the kotlin-repo-default profile to compose both plugins without duplicate skills, agents, or hooks.
forbiddenActions: Put generic Git, CI, PR, or shell automation skills inside kotlin-engineering.
Route Kotlin type-safety review through generic Git/CI primitives.
Let two selected plugins provide the same hook or agent in a profile.
cases.recoveryExpectation: Composition should keep Kotlin semantic work and generic delivery work in separate plugins while the profile validates that no duplicate owners are selected.
cases.notes: This is the main overlap case fixed by exposing kotlin-engineering as a Kotlin best-practices package and keeping delivery composition at the profile boundary.
cases.productiveOutcome: Kotlin semantic work and Git/CI delivery compose cleanly without duplicate owners or bloated plugin prompts.
verificationEvidence: kotlin-engineering owns Kotlin semantic and Gradle primitives only.
git-ci-operations owns branch, PR, CI, release, and shell automation primitives.

cases.prompt: Set up a repository to consume this marketplace, install plugins, and keep the resolved marketplace state locked and validated.

observedRoute.summary: Older Intelligence memories can route setup work toward deprecated APM, Node, Python, or package-manager-era flows.
observedRoute.risk: Agents use obsolete commands and miss the current source-of-truth split between marketplace source and CLI validation.

allowedActions: Prefer the current intelligence CLI for marketplace import, install, inspect, and validate workflows.
Treat slopsentral source files as canonical marketplace content.
Use repository-onboarding for consumer-repo setup and baseline marketplace guidance.
forbiddenActions: Route current setup through deprecated APM, NPM, Node, Python, or wrapper-era implementation paths.
Invent a new marketplace schema when import, install, and lock behavior already exist.
Vendor generated provider payloads into a consumer repository as the durable source of truth.
cases.recoveryExpectation: When older memory conflicts with current source, agents should re-check the source checkout and current intelligence CLI before acting.
cases.notes: This preserves current workflow attribution: slopsentral owns marketplace source; intelligence owns CLI validation and install flows.
cases.productiveOutcome: Repository setup uses the current marketplace CLI and source-of-truth split, avoiding obsolete package-manager paths.
verificationEvidence: Commands refer to the current intelligence marketplace workflow.
Consumer setup preserves source manifests and lock/install evidence instead of vendoring generated payloads.

cases.prompt: Install a slopsentral plugin globally, but the configured marketplace cache reports a missing plugin manifest.

observedRoute.summary: A setup failure can be misread as source corruption or fixed by hand-editing generated caches.
observedRoute.risk: Local caches diverge from source and the successful install path is not reproducible.

allowedActions: Inspect configured marketplace/cache state and confirm installed plugin state with the provider CLI.
Materialize a fresh marketplace snapshot from source when remote refresh fails.
Validate source and hydrated marketplace output before retrying installation.
forbiddenActions: Patch installed cache payloads as if they were source.
Edit generated provider output to satisfy one local install.
Stop after source validation without proving the actual install state when the request is global installation.
cases.recoveryExpectation: Treat cache and network failures as setup friction: refresh or rematerialize from source, validate, retry install, and verify installed state.
cases.notes: This avoids conflating consumer cache problems with marketplace source quality.
cases.productiveOutcome: Plugin install failures are recovered reproducibly from source instead of corrupting local caches.
verificationEvidence: The run inspects configured marketplace and installed plugin state.
A fresh source materialization and validation precede any retry when cache refresh fails.

cases.prompt: Create source-backed Kotlin concept docs, signature indexes, impact checks, and drift hooks for an OKF knowledge bundle.

observedRoute.summary: The request can be routed to generic documentation authoring or Kotlin implementation because it mentions docs and Kotlin.
observedRoute.risk: Generated knowledge pages lose code-source backing, signature evidence, and drift checks.

allowedActions: Use code-knowledge-base when the deliverable is an OKF bundle, signature index, code_sources impact check, or drift hook.
Use shared doc primitives only as supporting workflow inside the code-knowledge-base boundary.
Keep source-backed evidence explicit in generated knowledge artifacts.
forbiddenActions: Treat OKF generation as generic prose editing.
Route the work through Kotlin implementation loops when no code change is requested.
Drop code_sources or signature evidence from the output.
cases.recoveryExpectation: The code-knowledge-base metadata and routing case should distinguish source-backed knowledge work from general docs and Kotlin delivery.
cases.notes: This covers an intentional shared-skill overlap where the plugin boundary changes the expected output.
cases.productiveOutcome: Knowledge-base work leaves source-backed OKF artifacts with signature and drift evidence instead of generic prose.
verificationEvidence: Generated or updated knowledge artifacts retain code_sources or signature evidence.
The code-knowledge-base plugin boundary remains the primary route.

cases.prompt: Design persisted JSON and OpenAPI contracts with discriminated variants, typed errors, examples, and validation evidence.

observedRoute.summary: Type-safety language can route API boundary work into Kotlin engineering or general schema maintenance alone.
observedRoute.risk: Operation-level examples, OpenAPI review, and typed error modeling are missed.

allowedActions: Use api-contracts for JSON Schema and OpenAPI boundary design.
Use manage-json-schemas as a shared primitive inside the broader API contract workflow.
Validate examples, variant constraints, and operation contracts.
forbiddenActions: Route API boundary design to Kotlin implementation because both mention types.
Stop at standalone JSON Schema maintenance when OpenAPI operation contracts are in scope.
Promote unconstrained primitives where the schema can encode invalid states.
cases.recoveryExpectation: Shared schema skills should remain allowed overlap, but API contract prompts should load the api-contracts plugin when OpenAPI or operation boundaries are mentioned.
cases.notes: This protects a deliberate overlap from becoming an ownership ambiguity.
cases.productiveOutcome: API boundary work produces schema and OpenAPI contracts that encode invariants and examples at the correct boundary.
verificationEvidence: JSON Schema and OpenAPI examples or constraints are validated.
The api-contracts plugin owns the workflow when persisted API boundaries are in scope.

cases.prompt: Add a hook requirement file that tells the required-skill-read hook which skills must be read.

observedRoute.summary: Hook requirement data can be persisted as ad hoc JSON without a schema or reference checks.
observedRoute.risk: A hook silently points at a missing skill, stale schema, or non-marketplace-visible required primitive.

allowedActions: Use a schema-backed requirements file with explicit type and schemaVersion fields.
Validate required skills against canonical source frontmatter.
Fail source validation when the requirement references missing or hidden skills.
forbiddenActions: Persist hook requirements without a schema pointer.
Reference a required skill that is absent from source or hidden from marketplace exposure.
Treat hook adapter JSON as proof that its requirement payload is valid.
cases.recoveryExpectation: The source validator should block schema drift, missing required skills, and hook adapter command drift.
cases.notes: This case keeps hook data contracts aligned with the repo's schema-first rule.
cases.productiveOutcome: Hook requirements remain machine-checkable and cannot silently point at missing or hidden skills.
verificationEvidence: Requirement files include schema metadata and validate through source graph checks.
Referenced required skills exist in canonical source and are marketplace-visible.

cases.prompt: Audit marketplace-visible standalone skills for placeholder migration text and overlap with existing canonical skills.

observedRoute.summary: Placeholder skills can remain marketplace-visible because they are syntactically valid primitive directories.
observedRoute.risk: Agents load useless skills, waste context, and attribute capability to empty migration shells.

allowedActions: Quarantine placeholder-only skills from marketplace exposure.
Record deferred rewrite or removal decisions in the cleanup ledger.
Fail validation if marketplace-visible skills expose placeholder migration descriptions.
forbiddenActions: Keep placeholder-only skills in the standalone marketplace catalog.
Replace a real canonical skill with a placeholder duplicate.
Treat syntactic validity as quality readiness.
cases.recoveryExpectation: Primitive quality audit should either rewrite a placeholder into a real workflow or keep it out of marketplace exposure until it is ready.
cases.notes: This turns token-wasting placeholder exposure into a source validation failure.
cases.productiveOutcome: Team-shared marketplace listings contain usable primitives, while unfinished migration placeholders stay out of daily-driver routing.
verificationEvidence: Marketplace-visible skills have routeable descriptions rather than placeholder migration text.
Unfinished primitive candidates are handled through quality audit or consolidation manifests before promotion.
